---
title: "Feasibility of building a whole-cell mechanistic model of human platelet"
shorttitle: "Platelet whole-cell model"
author:
  - name: Steve Haigh
    affiliations:
      - University of Reading
    corresponding: true
    email: s.haigh@pgr.reading.ac.uk
runningauthor: "Haigh"
format:
  pnas-pdf:
    keep-tex: false
    classoption: [twocolumn]
abstract: |
  *[Abstract — ~200 words. One paragraph. Cover: the
  question, the methodology, the headline result, the broader
  claim.]*

  We present a calibrated whole-cell mechanistic model of human
  platelet cytosolic calcium signalling, built by adapting the
  Covert-Lab *wcEcoli* framework [@karr2012wcm; @macklin2020ecoli]
  to platelet biology. The model integrates twelve coupled
  mechanisms — three Gq-coupled receptors (P2Y1, PAR1, PAR4),
  the Gαq / PLCβ / PI-cycle cascade, IP3R-mediated dense-tubular-
  system (DTS) Ca²⁺ release, SERCA, PMCA, NCX, P2X1, store-
  operated entry (STIM1 + Orai1), mitochondrial MCU / NCLX
  shuttling, and cytosolic and luminal Ca²⁺ buffering — driven
  end-to-end by physiological agonist concentrations
  (1 nM thrombin, 10 µM ADP). *[State validation result:
  Dolan & Diamond 2014 Fig. 4 reproduction, 5/5 acceptance
  criteria, resting cyt 104 nM, resting DTS 235 µM.]*
  *[State the broader claim: first calibrated mass-balanced
  mechanistic platelet WCM; serves as substrate for downstream
  processes; in principle extensible to cross-disciplinary
  applications.]*
significance: |
  *[~120 words, PNAS-style. Aimed at non-specialists. Why does
  this matter? What new capability does this open up? Avoid
  jargon.]*

  *[Suggested arc: (1) platelet activation is a Ca²⁺ event,
  central to haemostasis and disease; (2) existing models
  treat individual pathways in isolation; (3) we built the
  first mass-balanced multi-process model with every protein
  counted and every reaction calibrated; (4) the model is now
  a substrate for asking mechanistic questions that cannot be
  addressed by isolated-pathway models — e.g. which extrusion
  pathway rate-limits cytosolic recovery, how mitochondrial
  buffering interacts with DTS refilling.]*
bibliography: mike-report-2026-05-14.bib
---

# Introduction

*[Section ~600 words. Cover: the biological question, the
modelling-framework choice, the gap this work fills.]*

**The biological question.** Platelet activation is initiated by
a cytosolic Ca²⁺ transient. *[Two to three sentences on the
agonist-to-Ca²⁺-peak cascade: agonist binds Gq-coupled receptors,
Gαq activates PLCβ, PIP₂ → IP₃ + DAG, IP₃ opens IP3R in the DTS
membrane (the platelet's ER-equivalent), and DTS Ca²⁺ floods
into the cytosol. Reference Dolan 2014 [@dolan2014model] and the
Mike group's PI-cycle paper [@mazet2020pi] as appropriate.]*

*[One paragraph on why a whole-cell rather than single-pathway
model: resting state is a multi-component balance; mechanistic
predictions require all components present and mass-balanced;
isolated pathway models cannot answer "what rate-limits
recovery" or "how does mitochondrial buffering interact with
DTS refill".]*

**The framework choice.** *[~250 words. Comparison of three
options, motivating wcEcoli.]*

- **COPASI** [@hoops2006copasi] — ODE/stochastic solver, SBML
  import, single-pathway focus. *[Note: excellent solver, no
  native mass-balance across compartments, no copy-number
  accounting, no listener / analysis pipeline.]*
- **SBML and BioModels** [@hucka2003sbml] — *[Note: format and
  catalogue, not engine. Each model in isolation. Re-use
  limited by integration cost.]*
- **wcEcoli** [@macklin2020ecoli] — *[Note: the only validated
  multi-process whole-cell model. Mass-balanced. Every protein
  counted. Compartmentalised. Listener / analysis framework.
  But: E. coli-specific biology, ~80 kloc, unmaintained since
  2022.]*

*[Closing paragraph: our approach was to fork wcEcoli, prune
all E. coli biology, and rebuild from the platelet literature.
We kept the simulation engine, state partitioning, and
analysis framework; replaced reconstruction and processes.
Cost: ~1 month of pruning; benefit: ~5,000 lines of validated
infrastructure that we did not need to write.]*

**Gap this work fills.** *[One paragraph. State clearly: no
prior calibrated multi-process platelet WCM exists. Dolan 2014
[@dolan2014model] is the closest comparator but treats Ca²⁺
handling at the FP level, with hand-fitted IP₃ forcing. Mazet
2020 [@mazet2020pi] covers the upstream PI-cycle but stops at
IP₃. We connect these with explicit downstream mechanisms and
validate end-to-end.]*

# Materials and Methods

*[Section ~1200 words. Cover: framework adaptation, biological-
content-build methodology, calibration discipline, sanity-check
protocol (including the k₃ story), software and reproducibility.]*

## Framework adaptation

*[~200 words. What was kept, what was removed, what was added.]*

*[Kept: `wholecell/` simulation engine, state classes
(BulkMolecules, UniqueMolecules, LocalEnvironment), listener /
analysis architecture, I/O format. Removed: all
`models/ecoli/`, all `reconstruction/ecoli/`, ParCa (parameter
calibration) pipeline, Cython performance modules (no longer
required at platelet timescales). Added: `models/platelet/`,
`reconstruction/platelet/`, `raw_data/` platelet tables.
Replaced wcEcoli's heavyweight ParCa step with a directly-
constructed `SimulationDataPlatelet` object.]*

## Biological content — methodology per mechanism

*[~400 words. Walk through the per-mechanism workflow. The
reader should leave knowing exactly what was done for each of
the twelve mechanisms.]*

*[Suggested arc: for each of the 12 mechanisms we followed a
fixed five-step process.]*

1. **Anchor paper selection.** *[Dolan & Diamond 2014 supplied
   the validation experiment (Fig. 4: IP₃-stimulated Ca²⁺
   transients with and without extracellular Ca²⁺) and
   resting-state targets (100 nM cyt, 250 µM DTS).]*
2. **Literature review for kinetics.** *[Primary sources for
   each mechanism — examples: deYoung-Keizer 1992
   [@dyk1992ip3r] / Li-Rinzel 1994 [@lirinzel1994] for IP3R
   gating; Purvis 2008 [@purvis2008model] (with corrections,
   see below) for SERCA; Caride 2007 [@caride2007pmca] for
   PMCA; Hoover & Lewis 2011 [@hoover2011soce] / Dolan 2014
   for SOCE. Mazet et al. 2020 [@mazet2020pi] for the PI cycle.]*
3. **Species enumeration.** *[Every Ca²⁺-binding or -gating
   protein conformational state becomes a tracked species with
   a compartment tag (e.g. `PMCA[pl]`, `SERCA_E1Ca[dts]`).
   Total ~50 species across the calcium pathway.]*
4. **Copy-number extraction.** *[Burkhart 2012
   [@burkhart2012proteome] supplied platelet-specific protein
   copy numbers from quantitative proteomics. Reference
   [@coller1995] for receptor counts.]*
5. **Rate-constant extraction.** *[Primary sources, with units
   normalised, and every value carrying a citation comment in
   code. Concentration units in µM; time in seconds.]*

*[Closing paragraph: each mechanism then enters the ODE right-
hand side as a small set of fluxes; the integrator
(`scipy.integrate.solve_ivp`, BDF method) advances species
counts on a 1-second outer timestep.]*

## Sanity-check discipline

*[~400 words. This is the methodologically interesting section.
Cover (a) the validation protocol, (b) the calibration-coupled
parameters, (c) the k₃ story.]*

**Validation gates.** *[Describe: each increment of the model
must pass (i) resting-state stability over 200 s, (ii) Dolan
Fig. 4 IP₃-transient reproduction, (iii) the 21-test unit
suite. Changes that break a gate are reverted.]*

**Calibration-coupled parameters.** *[Explain that some
parameters are mutually constrained — e.g. the IP3R single-
channel conductance γ\textsubscript{IP3R} is back-derived to
balance SERCA flux at the resting state. If SERCA constants
change, γ\textsubscript{IP3R} must be re-derived. Document this
as an explicit constructor-time assertion.]*

**The Purvis k₃ story.** *[~200-word narrative paragraph. This
is the methodologically interesting finding and should be told
as a story.]*

*[Suggested arc: (1) After implementing SERCA from Purvis 2008
Table 1, the resting state failed: cytosolic Ca²⁺ ran away or
collapsed depending on initial conditions. (2) Back-tracing the
ODE flux balance pointed at one rate constant, k₃, governing
the E₁P·Ca → E₂P·Ca phosphoryl-transfer step. (3) Cross-checking
Purvis 2008 Table 1 against the primary source it cited
(Dode 2002 [@dode2002serca]) revealed the Purvis value was the
reciprocal of the Dode value — a transcription error propagated
unchecked. (4) With the corrected value, the resting state
held. (5) Three other Purvis values were subsequently spot-
checked against primaries; one more rounding-induced drift was
found.]*

*[Closing observation: this episode is what underwrites the
methodological discipline of "every numerical value carries a
primary-source citation in the code, and primaries are
consulted directly when calibration fails". The pattern is
also a warning about AI-assisted parameter extraction: large
language models confidently reproduce review-paper values
without verifying upstream.]*

## Software and reproducibility

*[~150 words. Cover: language (Python 3.11), public repository
(`stevehaigh/platelet-wcm`), test suite, CI pipeline (GitHub
Actions), Docker images for runtime and webapp deployment, the
Dash webapp at `platelet-wcm.uksouth.azurecontainer.io` for
browsing simulation runs.]*

# Results

*[Section ~1200 words. Cover: model architecture, the validation
result, two or three specific findings.]*

## Model architecture

*[~250 words paragraph + Figure 1. Walk the reader through the
diagram top-to-bottom: agonist input, GPCRs in the plasma
membrane, Gαq cascade in the cytosol, PI cycle, IP3R on the
DTS membrane, the Ca²⁺-cyt hub, DTS lumen with multi-protein
buffering, mitochondrial shuttle, plasma membrane extrusion
(PMCA, NCX) and entry (Orai1, P2X1). Note that no hand-fitted
forcing remains in the calcium path: the model takes
physiological agonist concentrations as input and produces all
downstream behaviour mechanistically.]*

![Architecture of the platelet Ca²⁺ whole-cell model. Agonist
input drives three Gq-coupled receptors; the Gαq cascade
activates PLCβ, which hydrolyses PIP₂ to IP₃ and DAG; IP₃ opens
IP3R channels in the DTS membrane, releasing stored Ca²⁺; SERCA
returns Ca²⁺ to the DTS; PMCA and NCX extrude to the
extracellular space; SOCE refills the cytosol through Orai1;
P2X1 provides ATP-gated fast entry; mitochondria buffer
transiently via MCU and NCLX. Cytosolic and luminal buffering
by calmodulin, gelsolin (cyt) and calreticulin, HSP90B1, BiP,
CREC (DTS) is shown explicitly. Compartment boxes correspond
to PM, cytosol (with nested DTS and mitochondria), and
extracellular space. **[Figure to be replaced with hand-drawn
BioRender version for final manuscript.]**](../figures/v0.5/model-status-graphviz.png){#fig-architecture width=100%}

## Validation against Dolan & Diamond 2014

*[~400 words + Figure 2. The headline scientific result.]*

*[Suggested structure: (1) Describe the Dolan Fig. 4 experiment:
paired conditions with and without extracellular Ca²⁺, showing
IP₃-driven transients shaped by the presence or absence of
SOCE refill. (2) State the five Phase 3 acceptance criteria
(resting cyt 100 ± 10 nM, resting DTS within band, transient
peak height, transient duration, paired-condition difference).
(3) Report the model achieves 5/5 with resting cyt = 104 nM,
DTS = 235 µM, IP₃ = 50 nM, Gαq-active = 100. (4) Note this is
achieved without IP₃ forcing — driven by explicit upstream Gq
cascade.]*

![Free vs. bound Ca²⁺ during a 240-second IP₃-driven transient.
Cytosolic free Ca²⁺ rises from ~100 nM to ~500 nM and recovers
within 60 s; DTS free Ca²⁺ depletes by ~60 % during the peak
and refills via SOCE. Cytosolic and luminal buffer occupancy
track the free pools with clear coupling. **[Caption to be
refined: state which condition (with or without extracellular
Ca²⁺) is plotted and add panel labels if dual condition.]**](../figures/v0.5/ca-bound-free-v0.4.0.png){#fig-validation width=100%}

## Mechanistic findings

*[~500 words. Pick two or three specific findings that emerged
from having all components mechanistically present — the things
the model lets you say that an isolated pathway model could not.]*

*[Candidate findings to draft:*

1. ***PMCA Vmax rate-limits cytosolic Ca²⁺ recovery at low
   cytosolic concentrations.** Even with mitochondrial buffering
   active, the late tail of recovery is set by PMCA turnover.
   Testable prediction: pharmacological PMCA activation should
   accelerate recovery; PMCA inhibition should prolong Ca²⁺
   tails.*
2. ***MCU buffering alone does not accelerate DTS recovery** —
   sequestering Ca²⁺ into mitochondria removes it from the
   cytosol but does not return it to the DTS, so DTS refilling
   remains limited by SOCE. Testable prediction: mitochondrial-
   targeted Ca²⁺ uptake activators may actually prolong DTS
   overshoot rather than accelerate return to resting state.*
3. ***The DTS overshoots its resting level during recovery from
   sustained stimulation.** The model predicts a ~1 mM transient
   in DTS Ca²⁺ during the 1000–3000 s recovery phase, matching
   the long Ca²⁺ tails reported in real activated platelets.
   Whether this is biology or model artefact is an open question;
   it is a hypothesis the model generates that single-pathway
   models could not.]*

# Discussion

*[Section ~500 words. Cover: what the model lets you do,
limitations, future directions.]*

*[Suggested paragraphs: (1) The mechanistic-end-to-end property
— once a calibrated substrate exists, downstream questions
(granule release, integrin activation, platelet-CTC interactions)
attach naturally to it. (2) Limitations — coarse-grained
cytosolic buffering, lumped SOCE machinery, no Ca²⁺
microdomains. The deYoung-Keizer IP3R has a low-Ca²⁺
bistability artefact that emerges only at sustained stimulation
> 2000 s, documented but unresolved. (3) Open biology question
on Purvis SERCA Vmax — the rate constants imply ~5 cycles/s/pump
at resting cyt, which is ~2–5× higher than literature suggests
for SERCA3b at low substrate. Resolution awaits new primary-
source kinetics. (4) Methodological generalisation — the per-
mechanism workflow (anchor paper → literature → species →
counts → rates → cross-check) is portable to other single-cell
calibration problems.]*

*[Closing paragraph: the model is at v0.4.1, validated against
Dolan & Diamond 2014. Future work splits into near-term biology
(granule release, P2Y₁₂ inhibitory arm) and longer-term
architecture (a kinetics-as-data refactor to enable contributor
extensibility).]*

# Acknowledgements

*[Brief, ~50 words. Mention Mike Fry for upstream PI-cycle
framework; Covert Lab for the wcEcoli open-source release; any
specific advice from collaborators.]*

# References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::
